stimulus: Hoping to commemorate the 75th anniversary of statehood, ______blank up with a slogan that best reflected the territory’s pioneering spirit. The committee selected "Land of Enchantment," submitted by Las Cruces teacher Joseph Cisneros, as its winning entry.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: a contest organized by the New Mexico Statehood Committee would award $500 to a citizen who came
an award of $500 would go to a citizen in a contest organized by the New Mexico Statehood Committee for coming
the New Mexico Statehood Committee organized a contest that would award $500 to a citizen who came
$500 would be awarded to a citizen by the New Mexico Statehood Committee in a contest for coming

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-modifier placement. This choice makes the noun phrase "the New Mexico Statehood Committee" the subject of the sentence and places it immediately after the modifying phrase "Hoping...statehood." In doing so, this choice clearly establishes that the New Mexico Statehood Committee—and not another noun in the sentence—hoped to commemorate the 75th anniversary of statehood.

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier: placing "a contest" after the modifier illogically suggests that the contest hoped to commemorate the anniversary. Choice B is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier: placing "an award of $500" after the modifier illogically suggests that the award hoped to commemorate the anniversary. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier: placing "$500" after the modifier illogically suggests that $500 hoped to commemorate the anniversary.
